1	import AudioCaptureKit
     2	import AVFoundation
     3	import CoreAudio
     4	import CoreGraphics
     5	import Foundation
     6	import os
     7	
     8	/// Owns the AudioCaptureKit session and orchestrates the recording lifecycle.
     9	/// Reports state changes back via closures registered by the caller (RecordingViewModel).
    10	@MainActor
    11	final class RecordingService {
    12	    // MARK: - Callbacks
    13	
    14	    /// Called when recording state or elapsed duration changes.
    15	    /// Duration is `nil` on explicit control calls (pause/resume/stop start);
    16	    /// it carries the actual elapsed seconds when delivered by the capture delegate.
    17	    var onCaptureStateUpdate: ((RecordingUIState, TimeInterval?) -> Void)?
    18	    var onDiagnosticsUpdate: ((CaptureSessionDiagnostics) -> Void)?
    19	    var onLevelsUpdate: ((AudioLevels) -> Void)?
    20	    var onSystemAudioActiveChange: ((Bool) -> Void)?
    21	    var onError: ((String) -> Void)?
    22	    var onRecordingCompleted: ((LocalRecording) -> Void)?
    23	    var onAvailableMicsUpdated: (([AudioSource], String?) -> Void)?
    24	    var onBluetoothConflict: ((Bool, String?) -> Void)?
    25	    var onSystemAudioPermittedChange: ((Bool) -> Void)?
    26	    var onMicDisconnectedDuringRecording: (() -> Void)?
    27	    var onDeviceChanged: (() -> Void)?
    28	
    29	    // MARK: - Internal
    30	
    31	    var recordingsDirectory: URL {
    32	        let dir = FileManager.default.urls(for: .documentDirectory, in: .userDomainMask)[0]
    33	            .appendingPathComponent("MacOSSample-Recordings", isDirectory: true)
    34	        try? FileManager.default.createDirectory(at: dir, withIntermediateDirectories: true)
    35	        return dir
    36	    }
    37	
    38	    // MARK: - Private
    39	
    40	    private var session: CompositeCaptureSession?
    41	    private let delegateAdapter = CaptureDelegateAdapter()
    42	    private var deviceChangeListenerInstalled = false
    43	    private var systemAudioAvailableAtStart = false
    44	    private var currentRecordingState: RecordingUIState = .idle
    45	    private var selectedMicID: String?
    46	    private var availableMics: [AudioSource] = []
    47	    private let logger = Logger(subsystem: AppConstants.appBundleID, category: "RecordingService")
    48	
    49	    // MARK: - Audio Sources
    50	
    51	    func loadAudioSources(currentMicID: String?) async {
    52	        onSystemAudioPermittedChange?(CGPreflightScreenCaptureAccess())
    53	        await refreshAudioSources(currentMicID: currentMicID)
    54	        installDeviceChangeListener()
    55	    }
    56	
    57	    func updateSelectedMic(_ micID: String?) {
    58	        selectedMicID = micID
    59	        checkBluetoothRoutingConflict()
    60	    }
    61	
    62	    func isMicStillAvailable(_ deviceUID: String) -> Bool {
    63	        let discoverySession = AVCaptureDevice.DiscoverySession(
    64	            deviceTypes: [.microphone, .external],
    65	            mediaType: .audio,
    66	            position: .unspecified
    67	        )
    68	        return discoverySession.devices.contains { $0.uniqueID == deviceUID }
    69	    }
    70	
    71	    // MARK: - Recording Controls
    72	
    73	    func startRecording(encryptionEnabled: Bool, debugEnableMic: Bool, debugEnableSystem: Bool) async {
    74	        logger.info("Starting recording – system audio: \(self.systemAudioAvailableAtStart)")
    75	
    76	        let encryptor: RecordingEncryptor? = encryptionEnabled ? RecordingEncryptor() : nil
    77	        let config = CaptureConfiguration(
    78	            sampleRate: 48000,
    79	            bitDepth: 16,
    80	            channels: 2,
    81	            encryptor: encryptor,
    82	            outputDirectory: recordingsDirectory,
    83	            micDeviceID: selectedMicID,
    84	            enableMicCapture: debugEnableMic,
    85	            enableSystemCapture: debugEnableSystem,
    86	            mixingStrategy: .separated
    87	        )
    88	
    89	        let captureSession = CompositeCaptureSession(configuration: config)
    90	        self.session = captureSession
    91	        configureDelegateCallbacks()
    92	        captureSession.delegate = delegateAdapter
    93	
    94	        do {
    95	            try captureSession.configure(config)
    96	            try await captureSession.startCapture()
    97	            currentRecordingState = .recording
    98	            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.recording, nil)
    99	            onSystemAudioActiveChange?(systemAudioAvailableAtStart)
   100	            logger.info("Recording started – systemAudioActive: \(self.systemAudioAvailableAtStart)")
   101	        } catch {
   102	            logger.error("Failed to start recording: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   103	            onError?("Failed to start recording: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   104	            currentRecordingState = .idle
   105	            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.idle, nil)
   106	            onSystemAudioActiveChange?(false)
   107	        }
   108	    }
   109	
   110	    func pauseRecording() {
   111	        guard let session else { return }
   112	        do {
   113	            try session.pauseCapture()
   114	            currentRecordingState = .paused
   115	            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.paused, nil)
   116	            logger.info("Recording paused")
   117	        } catch {
   118	            logger.error("Failed to pause: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   119	            onError?("Failed to pause: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   120	        }
   121	    }
   122	
   123	    func resumeRecording() {
   124	        guard let session else { return }
   125	        do {
   126	            try session.resumeCapture()
   127	            currentRecordingState = .recording
   128	            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.recording, nil)
   129	            logger.info("Recording resumed")
   130	        } catch {
   131	            logger.error("Failed to resume: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   132	            onError?("Failed to resume: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   133	        }
   134	    }
   135	
   136	    func stopRecording() async {
   137	        guard let session else { return }
   138	        do {
   139	            let result = try await session.stopCapture()
   140	            logger.info("Recording stopped: \(result.fileURL.lastPathComponent)")
   141	
   142	            let recording = LocalRecording(
   143	                id: result.metadata.id,
   144	                fileURL: result.fileURL,
   145	                duration: result.duration,
   146	                createdAt: Date(),
   147	                isEncrypted: result.metadata.isEncrypted,
   148	                checksum: result.checksum,
   149	                channelLayout: result.metadata.channelLayout,
   150	                isUploaded: false
   151	            )
   152	            onRecordingCompleted?(recording)
   153	            currentRecordingState = .idle
   154	            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.idle, nil)
   155	        } catch {
   156	            logger.error("Failed to stop recording: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   157	            onError?("Failed to stop recording: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   158	            currentRecordingState = .idle
   159	            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.idle, nil)
   160	        }
   161	        onSystemAudioActiveChange?(false)
   162	        self.session = nil
   163	    }

   338	        let status = AudioObjectGetPropertyData(

# AudioCaptureKit: Separate Audio Streams — Design Document

_Created: 2026-03-03_
_Status: READY FOR IMPLEMENTATION_
_Target repo: pablo-health/AudioCaptureKit_

---

## 1. Context

AudioCaptureKit is a first-party Pablo framework used in Pablo Companion (and potentially other products) to record therapy sessions on macOS. Currently it captures mic + system audio and writes both streams into a single mixed WAV file.

The Pablo transcription pipeline needs these streams **separated** so that:
- The mic file (therapist's voice) can be transcribed with a known speaker identity
- The system audio file (remote participant voices) can be transcribed and diarized independently
- Stereo system audio is preserved for high-quality human playback

AudioCaptureKit's job is simply to **capture at the best quality it can and write separate files**. It does not resample, mix, or do any ML processing. Downstream consumers (Rust core, ASR pipeline) handle resampling and channel manipulation.

---

## 2. Current API (as observed in pablo-companion)

```swift
// CaptureConfiguration
let config = CaptureConfiguration(
    sampleRate: 48000,
    bitDepth: 16,
    channels: 2,           // currently mixes mic + system into 2-ch file
    encryptor: encryptor,
    outputDirectory: recordingsDirectory,
    micDeviceID: selectedMicID,
    enableMicCapture: true,
    enableSystemCapture: true
)

// RecordingResult (returned by stopCapture())
result.fileURL        // single WAV file (mic + system mixed)
result.duration       // TimeInterval
result.metadata.id    // UUID
result.metadata.isEncrypted // Bool
result.checksum       // String (integrity check)
```

---

## 3. Requirements

### 3.1 New Behavior: Separate Output Files

When configured for separate output, AudioCaptureKit must write **two files**:

| File | Channels | Content | Format |
|------|----------|---------|--------|
| Mic file | **1 (mono)** | Microphone input only — no system audio | 48 kHz, 16-bit PCM WAV |
| System audio file | **2 (stereo)** | System/loopback audio only — no mic | 48 kHz, 16-bit PCM WAV |

**Key principle:** Capture at full quality, do not downsample. Consumers decide what to do with the audio.

### 3.2 File Naming

Use the same base naming convention as the existing single-file mode, with suffixes:

```
<existing_base_name>_mic.wav       // microphone
<existing_base_name>_system.wav    // system audio
```

Example: if the existing convention produces `recording-2026-03-03T14-30-00.wav`, produce:
```
recording-2026-03-03T14-30-00_mic.wav
recording-2026-03-03T14-30-00_system.wav
```

### 3.3 Encryption

If an encryptor is provided, **both files** are encrypted independently. Each file gets its own encrypted output.

### 3.4 Checksums

Each file gets its own checksum for integrity verification (HIPAA compliance requires this).

### 3.5 Backward Compatibility

The default behavior is **unchanged**. Existing callers passing no `streamMode` get the current single-file combined output. No existing code in pablo-companion or other consumers should break.

### 3.6 Partial Capture

Handle gracefully:
- If only mic capture is enabled → produce only mic file
- If only system capture is enabled → produce only system file
- If neither system audio is available (user hasn't granted permission) → proceed with mic-only, no system file

---

## 4. Proposed API Changes

### 4.1 New Enum: `AudioStreamMode`

```swift
/// Controls whether AudioCaptureKit mixes audio streams into one file
/// or writes each source to a separate file.
public enum AudioStreamMode: Sendable {
    /// Mic and system audio are mixed into a single stereo WAV file.
    /// This is the existing behavior and the default.
    case combined

    /// Mic and system audio are written as separate files.
    /// Mic: mono WAV.  System audio: stereo WAV.
    /// fileURL in RecordingResult points to the mic file.
    /// systemAudioFileURL in RecordingResult points to the system audio file.
    case separateFiles
}
```

### 4.2 Updated `CaptureConfiguration`

Add one new property. All existing properties and their defaults are unchanged.

```swift
public struct CaptureConfiguration: Sendable {
    // --- EXISTING PROPERTIES (unchanged) ---
    public var sampleRate: Int
    public var bitDepth: Int
    public var channels: Int
    public var encryptor: (any RecordingEncryptor)?
    public var outputDirectory: URL
    public var micDeviceID: String?
    public var enableMicCapture: Bool
    public var enableSystemCapture: Bool

    // --- NEW PROPERTY ---
    /// Controls whether mic and system audio are written to a single file
    /// (combined, existing behavior) or two separate files.
    /// Default: .combined (preserves backward compatibility)
    public var streamMode: AudioStreamMode = .combined

    // Existing init remains unchanged — new property uses default value
}
```

### 4.3 Updated `RecordingResult`

Add one new optional property. All existing properties are unchanged.

```swift
public struct RecordingResult: Sendable {
    // --- EXISTING PROPERTIES (unchanged) ---
    /// Primary output file.
    /// - In .combined mode: the single mixed WAV file (existing behavior).
    /// - In .separateFiles mode: the microphone-only WAV file.
    public let fileURL: URL

    public let duration: TimeInterval
    public let metadata: RecordingMetadata
    public let checksum: String  // checksum of fileURL

    // --- NEW PROPERTIES ---
    /// System audio output file. Only present when streamMode == .separateFiles
    /// AND system audio was successfully captured.
    /// nil in .combined mode or if no system audio was captured.
    public let systemAudioFileURL: URL?

    /// Checksum of systemAudioFileURL. nil when systemAudioFileURL is nil.
    public let systemAudioChecksum: String?
}
```

### 4.4 No Other API Changes

- `CompositeCaptureSession` public interface is unchanged
- `AudioCaptureDelegate` protocol is unchanged
- `CaptureState`, `AudioLevels`, `CaptureError`, `AudioSource` are unchanged
- All existing initializers continue to work

---

## 5. Internal Implementation Guidance

> Note: This section is for the implementer. The public API is what matters; implementation details may vary.

### 5.1 Recording Architecture

In `.combined` mode (existing): a single `AVAudioFile` or similar writer receives mixed audio.

In `.separateFiles` mode (new):
- Maintain two separate audio writers: one for mic, one for system audio
- Write mic samples only to the mic writer
- Write system audio samples only to the system audio writer
- Do not cross-contaminate streams

### 5.2 Channel Configuration

In `.separateFiles` mode, the `channels` parameter in `CaptureConfiguration` applies to the **combined** mode only (for backward compat). In separate mode:
- Mic file: always 1 channel (mono)
- System file: always 2 channels (stereo)

The implementer may choose to ignore the `channels` value in `.separateFiles` mode or derive the channel count from the capture source.

### 5.3 Silence / No Capture

In `.separateFiles` mode:
- If `enableMicCapture = false` → no mic file produced → `fileURL` should still be valid but contains silence or an empty duration, OR return a meaningful error. **Preferred:** if mic is the primary file, treat a disabled mic as an error in `.separateFiles` mode.
- If `enableSystemCapture = false` OR system audio is unavailable → `systemAudioFileURL = nil`, `systemAudioChecksum = nil`. This is not an error.

### 5.4 Timing Alignment

Both files must share the same start timestamp and session ID so that consumers can align them. The `metadata.id` (UUID) must be identical in both files' metadata.

### 5.5 Encryption

If an encryptor is provided:
1. Write the raw WAV to a temp file
2. Encrypt the temp file → final output file
3. Delete the temp file
4. Repeat for each file (mic, system) independently

### 5.6 Checksum

Compute checksum over the final output file bytes (after encryption if encrypted). Use the same checksum algorithm as the existing implementation.

---

## 6. How pablo-companion Uses This

After this change, `RecordingService.swift` in pablo-companion will be updated to:

```swift
let config = CaptureConfiguration(
    sampleRate: 48000,
    bitDepth: 16,
    channels: 2,         // only relevant for .combined (ignored in .separateFiles)
    encryptor: encryptor,
    outputDirectory: recordingsDirectory,
    micDeviceID: selectedMicID,
    enableMicCapture: true,
    enableSystemCapture: true,
    streamMode: .separateFiles   // NEW
)
```

And `RecordingService` will receive:
```swift
let result = try await session.stopCapture()
result.fileURL              // mic file (therapist)
result.systemAudioFileURL   // system audio file (client(s)), may be nil
result.checksum             // mic file checksum
result.systemAudioChecksum  // system audio checksum, may be nil
```

The pablo-companion `LocalRecording` model will be updated to store both URLs. The AudioCaptureKit design doc does not need to specify those downstream changes.

---

## 7. Testing Requirements

### 7.1 Unit Tests

- `.combined` mode produces a single file (existing behavior unchanged)
- `.separateFiles` mode with both sources enabled: produces two files, mic is mono, system is stereo
- `.separateFiles` mode with system audio unavailable: produces mic file only, `systemAudioFileURL` is nil
- `.separateFiles` mode with encryption: both files are encrypted, checksums are valid
- File naming: mic file ends with `_mic.wav`, system file ends with `_system.wav`
- Both files share the same `metadata.id`
- Duration of both files matches the session duration

### 7.2 Manual / Integration Tests

- Record a real session (requires mic + screen recording permission)
- Verify mic file contains only mic audio (no echo of system audio)
- Verify system file contains only system audio (no mic bleed)
- Verify both files play back correctly in QuickTime / AVFoundation
- Verify checksum validation passes for both files
- Verify encrypted files decrypt correctly

---

## 8. Definition of Done

- [ ] `AudioStreamMode` enum is public and documented
- [ ] `CaptureConfiguration.streamMode` added with default `.combined`
- [ ] `RecordingResult.systemAudioFileURL` and `systemAudioChecksum` added as optionals
- [ ] `.combined` mode behavior is byte-for-byte identical to the existing implementation (no regression)
- [ ] `.separateFiles` mode produces correct mono mic file and stereo system file
- [ ] Encryption works for both files in `.separateFiles` mode
- [ ] File naming follows `_mic` / `_system` suffix convention
- [ ] Both files share the same `metadata.id`
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] `make check` (or equivalent) passes with zero warnings

Author: Kurt Niemi <kurtn@pablo.health>
Date:   Tue Mar 3 21:34:17 2026 -0500

     Add channel-based diarization support (#24)
    
    * Add channel-based diarization support
    
    Introduces MixingStrategy, per-channel buffer callbacks, raw PCM sidecar
    export, and ChannelLayout metadata — enabling speaker diarization without ML
    by leveraging the structural separation of mic vs. system audio at capture time.
    
    ## New types (Swift + Rust, in sync)
    
    - `MixingStrategy` enum: `blended` (default, preserves existing behavior),
      `separated` (mic on L, system mono-fold on R), `multichannel` (reserved)
    - `ChannelBuffers`: raw Float32 per-channel samples delivered before mixing
    - `ChannelLayout`: records actual WAV channel layout in `RecordingMetadata`
      for downstream decoders; defaults to `.blended` when missing (backward compat)
    
    ## Configuration changes
    
    - `CaptureConfiguration.mixingStrategy` — controls WAV channel layout (default `.blended`)
    - `CaptureConfiguration.exportRawPCM` — writes `_mic.pcm` / `_system.pcm` sidecars (default false)
    - Channel count validation relaxed to 1–4 (was 1–2); channels 3–4 reserved
    
    ## StereoMixer
    
    - New `mix(mic:system:strategy:)` replaces direct calls to `mixMicWithStereoSystem`
    - New `separateChannels`: Left = mic, Right = (sysL + sysR) / 2 (mono-fold preserving both channels)

runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      mac: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.mac }}
      core: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.core }}
      has-cargo: ${{ steps.cargo-check.outputs.exists }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@de90cc6fb38fc0963ad72b210f1f284cd68cea36 # v3.0.2
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            mac:
              - 'mac/**'
            core:
              - 'core/**'
      - name: Check for core/Cargo.toml
        id: cargo-check
        run: |
          if [ -f core/Cargo.toml ]; then
            echo "exists=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "exists=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

  swift-lint:
    name: Swift Lint
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.mac == 'true'
--
          xcodebuild -project mac/PabloCompanion.xcodeproj \
            -scheme Pablo \
            -destination 'platform=macOS' \
            -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath mac/SourcePackages \
            $DISABLE_PKGS \
            CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" \
            CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO \
            CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO \
            build | xcpretty

  rust-lint:
    name: Rust Lint
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.core == 'true' && needs.changes.outputs.has-cargo == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@779680da715d629ac1d338a641029a2f4372abb5 # v2.8.2

      - name: Check formatting
        working-directory: core
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  changes:
    name: Detect changes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      mac: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.mac }}
      core: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.core }}
      has-cargo: ${{ steps.cargo-check.outputs.exists }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@de90cc6fb38fc0963ad72b210f1f284cd68cea36 # v3.0.2
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            mac:
              - 'mac/**'
            core:
              - 'core/**'
      - name: Check for core/Cargo.toml
        id: cargo-check
        run: |
          if [ -f core/Cargo.toml ]; then
            echo "exists=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "exists=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

  swift-lint:
    name: Swift Lint
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.mac == 'true'
    runs-on: macos-26
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - name: Install SwiftLint
        run: |
          SWIFTLINT_VERSION=0.63.2
          curl -sL "https://github.com/realm/SwiftLint/releases/download/${SWIFTLINT_VERSION}/portable_swiftlint.zip" -o swiftlint.zip
          unzip -q swiftlint.zip -d swiftlint
          echo "$PWD/swiftlint" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Install SwiftFormat
        run: |
          SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION=0.59.1
          curl -sL "https://github.com/nicklockwood/SwiftFormat/releases/download/${SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION}/swiftformat.zip" -o swiftformat.zip
          unzip -q swiftformat.zip
          chmod +x swiftformat
          echo "$PWD" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: SwiftLint
        run: swiftlint lint --strict --config mac/.swiftlint.yml mac/PabloCompanion/

      - name: SwiftFormat
        run: swiftformat --lint --config mac/.swiftformat mac/PabloCompanion/

  swift-build:
    name: Swift Build
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.mac == 'true' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
    runs-on: macos-26
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - name: Cache SPM dependencies
        id: spm-cache
        uses: actions/cache@cdf6c1fa76f9f475f3d7449005a359c84ca0f306 # v5.0.3
        with:
          path: mac/SourcePackages
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-spm-${{ hashFiles('mac/PabloCompanion.xcodeproj/project.xcworkspace/xcshareddata/swiftpm/Package.resolved') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-spm-

      - name: Build
        run: |
          DISABLE_PKGS=${{ steps.spm-cache.outputs.cache-hit == 'true' && '-disableAutomaticPackageResolution' || '' }}
          xcodebuild -project mac/PabloCompanion.xcodeproj \
            -scheme Pablo \
            -destination 'platform=macOS' \

content.text:
It's a warning at 400, error at 600. SwiftLint runs with `--strict` in CI, which promotes warnings to errors — so 403 lines is failing strict mode even though it's well under the hard limit.

The 400-line warning threshold is our own config, not a documented industry standard. The CLAUDE.md says "keep view files under 300 lines" which is even stricter — that was a guideline I wrote as a soft target for readability, not a hard rule.

A few options:

1. **Bump the warning to 450 or 500** — honest acknowledgment that 400 is just a number, and 403 is fine
2. **Split the file** — if the file has a natural seam (e.g. a large preview block, or a helper type that could live elsewhere)
3. **Leave it at 400 and just split the 3 lines** — sometimes the split is artificial and not worth it

Which file is it? That'll tell us if there's a natural seam or if bumping the threshold is the right call.

Right now `swift-test` and `swift-lint` both only need `changes` — they run in parallel with no dependency between them. CodeQL runs independently too.

**On the 400-line limit:** it's ours, not from Apple or SwiftLint defaults (SwiftLint's built-in default is 400 warning / 1000 error). 403 is not a meaningful difference. RecordingService is a coherent unit — pulling out `CaptureDelegateAdapter` and `WeakSendableBox` into their own files would be artificial since they're private types that exist purely to serve it.

My recommendation: **bump the warning to 450**. That's honest. If we ever hit 450 it's worth looking at, but 403 is fine.

**On the CI gate:** yes, `swift-test` should gate on `swift-lint` passing. CodeQL is trickier — it's in a separate workflow and has a security purpose, so failing lint shouldn't block it (you still want security analysis even on messy code).

    63→        let discoverySession = AVCaptureDevice.DiscoverySession(
    64→            deviceTypes: [.microphone, .external],
    65→            mediaType: .audio,
    66→            position: .unspecified
    67→        )

    71→    // MARK: - Recording Controls

    73→    func startRecording(encryptionEnabled: Bool, debugEnableMic: Bool, debugEnableSystem: Bool) async {

    75→
    76→        let encryptor: RecordingEncryptor? = encryptionEnabled ? RecordingEncryptor() : nil
    77→        let config = CaptureConfiguration(
    78→            sampleRate: 48000,
    79→            bitDepth: 16,
    80→            channels: 2,
    81→            encryptor: encryptor,
    82→            outputDirectory: recordingsDirectory,
    83→            micDeviceID: selectedMicID,
    84→            enableMicCapture: debugEnableMic,
    85→            enableSystemCapture: debugEnableSystem,
    86→            mixingStrategy: .separated,
    87→            exportRawPCM: true
    88→        )

    92→        configureDelegateCallbacks()
    93→        captureSession.delegate = delegateAdapter
    94→

    96→            try captureSession.configure(config)
    97→            try await captureSession.startCapture()
    98→            currentRecordingState = .recording
    99→            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.recording, nil)
   100→            onSystemAudioActiveChange?(systemAudioAvailableAtStart)
   101→            logger.info("Recording started – systemAudioActive: \(self.systemAudioAvailableAtStart)")
   102→        } catch {
   103→            logger.error("Failed to start recording: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   104→            onError?("Failed to start recording: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   105→            currentRecordingState = .idle
   106→            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.idle, nil)
   107→            onSystemAudioActiveChange?(false)
   108→        }
   109→    }
   110→
   111→    func pauseRecording() {
   112→        guard let session else { return }
   113→        do {
   114→            try session.pauseCapture()
   115→            currentRecordingState = .paused
   116→            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.paused, nil)
   117→            logger.info("Recording paused")
   118→        } catch {

   121→        }
   122→    }

   124→    func resumeRecording() {
   125→        guard let session else { return }

   127→            try session.resumeCapture()
   128→            currentRecordingState = .recording
   129→            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.recording, nil)
   130→            logger.info("Recording resumed")

   133→            onError?("Failed to resume: \(error.localizedDescription)")

   137→    func stopRecording() async {

   140→            let result = try await session.stopCapture()
   141→            logger.info("Recording stopped: \(result.fileURL.lastPathComponent)")
   142→
   143→            let recording = LocalRecording(
   144→                id: result.metadata.id,
   145→                fileURL: result.fileURL,
   146→                duration: result.duration,
   147→                createdAt: Date(),
   148→                isEncrypted: result.metadata.isEncrypted,
   149→                checksum: result.checksum,
   150→                channelLayout: result.metadata.channelLayout,
   151→                isUploaded: false,
   152→                micPCMFileURL: result.rawPCMFileURLs.indices.contains(0) ? result.rawPCMFileURLs[0] : nil,
   153→                systemAudioPCMFileURL: result.rawPCMFileURLs.indices.contains(1) ? result.rawPCMFileURLs[1] : nil

   157→            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.idle, nil)

   159→            logger.error("Failed to stop recording: \(error.localizedDescription)")

   162→            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.idle, nil)
   163→        }

   165→        self.session = nil
   166→    }

   168→    // MARK: - Private Helpers
   169→
   170→    private func refreshAudioSources(currentMicID: String?) async {
   171→        let tempSession = CompositeCaptureSession(
   172→            configuration: CaptureConfiguration(outputDirectory: recordingsDirectory)
   173→        )
   174→        do {
   175→            let sources = try await tempSession.availableAudioSources()
   176→            availableMics = sources.filter { $0.type == .mic }
   177→            let systemSources = sources.filter { $0.type == .system }
   178→            systemAudioAvailableAtStart = !systemSources.isEmpty
   179→
   180→            let currentMicStillAvailable = currentMicID
   181→                .map { id in availableMics.contains(where: { $0.id == id }) } ?? false
   182→            let newSelectedMicID = currentMicStillAvailable
   183→                ? currentMicID
   184→                : availableMics.first(where: { $0.isDefault })?.id ?? availableMics.first?.id
   185→            selectedMicID = newSelectedMicID
   186→
   187→            logger.info("Audio sources refreshed: \(self.availableMics.count) mic(s)")
   188→            logger.info("System audio available: \(self.systemAudioAvailableAtStart)")
   189→            onAvailableMicsUpdated?(availableMics, newSelectedMicID)
   190→            checkBluetoothRoutingConflict()
   191→        } catch {
   192→            logger.error("Failed to load audio sources: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   193→            onError?("Failed to load audio sources: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   194→        }
   195→    }
   196→
   197→    private func installDeviceChangeListener() {
   198→        guard !deviceChangeListenerInstalled else { return }
   199→
   200→        var address = AudioObjectPropertyAddress(
   201→            mSelector: kAudioHardwarePropertyDevices,
   202→            mScope: kAudioObjectPropertyScopeGlobal,
   203→            mElement: kAudioObjectPropertyElementMain
   204→        )
   205→
   206→        let weakSelf = WeakSendableBox(self)
   207→        let listenerBlock: @Sendable (UInt32, UnsafePointer<AudioObjectPropertyAddress>) -> Void = { _, _ in
   208→            DispatchQueue.main.async {
   209→                Task { @MainActor in
   210→                    guard let service = weakSelf.value else { return }
   211→                    service.handleDeviceChange()
   212→                    await service.refreshAudioSources(currentMicID: service.selectedMicID)
   213→                }
   214→            }
   215→        }
   216→
   217→        let status = AudioObjectAddPropertyListenerBlock(
   218→            AudioObjectID(kAudioObjectSystemObject),
   219→            &address,
   220→            nil,
   221→            listenerBlock
   222→        )
   223→
   224→        if status == noErr {
   225→            deviceChangeListenerInstalled = true
   226→            logger.info("Audio device change listener installed")
   227→        } else {

   229→        }
   230→    }
   231→
   232→    private func handleDeviceChange() {
   233→        logger.info("Audio device change detected, refreshing sources")
   234→
   235→        if currentRecordingState == .recording || currentRecordingState == .paused {
   236→            let micDisconnected = selectedMicID.map { !isMicStillAvailable($0) } ?? false
   237→            if micDisconnected, let micID = selectedMicID {
   238→                logger.warning("Selected mic \(micID) disconnected during recording, stopping")
   239→                Task { await stopRecording() }
   240→                onMicDisconnectedDuringRecording?()
   241→            } else {
   242→                logger.info("Device change during recording, but selected mic still available")
   243→            }
   244→        }
   245→
   246→        onDeviceChanged?()
   247→    }
   248→
   249→    private func configureDelegateCallbacks() {
   250→        delegateAdapter.onStateChange = { [weak self] state in
   251→            Task { @MainActor in
   252→                self?.handleCaptureState(state)
   253→            }
   254→        }
   255→        delegateAdapter.onLevelsUpdate = { [weak self] levels in
   256→            Task { @MainActor in
   257→                self?.onLevelsUpdate?(levels)
   258→            }
   259→        }
   260→        delegateAdapter.onError = { [weak self] error in
   261→            Task { @MainActor in
   262→                guard let self else { return }
   263→                self.logger.error("Capture error: \(error.localizedDescription)")
   264→                if error.isSystemAudioConfigError {
   265→                    self.onSystemAudioActiveChange?(false)
   266→                    self.logger.warning("System audio not available, recording mic only")
   267→                }
   268→                self.onError?(error.localizedDescription)
   269→            }
   270→        }
   271→    }
   272→
   273→    private func handleCaptureState(_ state: CaptureState) {
   274→        logger.debug("Capture state: \(String(describing: state))")
   275→        switch state {
   276→        case .idle:
   277→            currentRecordingState = .idle
   278→            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.idle, nil)
   279→        case let .capturing(elapsed):
   280→            currentRecordingState = .recording
   281→            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.recording, elapsed)
   282→            if let session {
   283→                onDiagnosticsUpdate?(session.diagnostics)
   284→            }
   285→        case let .paused(elapsed):
   286→            currentRecordingState = .paused
   287→            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.paused, elapsed)
   288→        case .stopping:
   289→            currentRecordingState = .idle
   290→            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.idle, nil)
   291→            if let session {
   292→                onDiagnosticsUpdate?(session.diagnostics)
   293→            }
   294→        case let .failed(error):
   295→            currentRecordingState = .idle
   296→            onCaptureStateUpdate?(.idle, nil)
   297→            onSystemAudioActiveChange?(false)
   298→            onError?(error.localizedDescription)
   299→        default:
   300→            break
   301→        }
   302→    }
   303→
   304→    private func checkBluetoothRoutingConflict() {
   305→        guard let selectedMicID,
   306→              let mic = availableMics.first(where: { $0.id == selectedMicID }),
   307→              mic.transportType == .bluetooth || mic.transportType == .bluetoothLE
   308→        else {
   309→            onBluetoothConflict?(false, nil)
   310→            return
   311→        }
   312→
   313→        guard let outputUID = Self.defaultOutputDeviceUID() else {
   314→            onBluetoothConflict?(false, nil)
   315→            return
   316→        }
   317→
   318→        let micBase = selectedMicID.components(separatedBy: ":").first ?? selectedMicID
   319→        let outputBase = outputUID.components(separatedBy: ":").first ?? outputUID
   320→
   321→        if micBase == outputBase {
   322→            let recommendation =
   323→                "Using \(mic.name) as mic while it's also the audio output forces HFP mode (mono ~16 kHz). "
   324→                    + "This is fine for speech recognition, but audio quality will drop. "
   325→                    + "Switch to the built-in mic for higher quality, or continue if convenience matters more."
   326→            onBluetoothConflict?(true, recommendation)
   327→        } else {
   328→            onBluetoothConflict?(false, nil)
   329→        }
   330→    }
   331→
   332→    private static func defaultOutputDeviceUID() -> String? {
   333→        var deviceID: AudioDeviceID = 0
   334→        var size = UInt32(MemoryLayout<AudioDeviceID>.size)
   335→        var address = AudioObjectPropertyAddress(
   336→            mSelector: kAudioHardwarePropertyDefaultOutputDevice,
   337→            mScope: kAudioObjectPropertyScopeGlobal,
   338→            mElement: kAudioObjectPropertyElementMain
   339→        )
   340→
   341→        let status = AudioObjectGetPropertyData(
   342→            AudioObjectID(kAudioObjectSystemObject),
   343→            &address, 0, nil, &size, &deviceID
   344→        )
   345→        guard status == noErr else { return nil }

   347→        address.mSelector = kAudioDevicePropertyDeviceUID
   348→        var uid: Unmanaged<CFString>?
   349→        size = UInt32(MemoryLayout<Unmanaged<CFString>?>.size)
   350→        let uidStatus = AudioObjectGetPropertyData(
   351→            deviceID, &address, 0, nil, &size, &uid
   352→        )
   353→        guard uidStatus == noErr, let uid = uid?.takeUnretainedValue() else { return nil }
   354→        return uid as String
   355→    }
   356→}

   358→// MARK: - Delegate Adapter
   359→
   360→/// Bridges the non-isolated AudioCaptureDelegate callbacks to MainActor closures.
   361→private final class CaptureDelegateAdapter: AudioCaptureDelegate, @unchecked Sendable {
   362→    var onStateChange: (@Sendable (CaptureState) -> Void)?
   363→    var onLevelsUpdate: (@Sendable (AudioLevels) -> Void)?
   364→    var onError: (@Sendable (CaptureError) -> Void)?
   365→
   366→    func captureSession(
   367→        _: any AudioCaptureSession,
   368→        didChangeState state: CaptureState
   369→    ) {
   370→        onStateChange?(state)
   371→    }
   372→
   373→    func captureSession(
   374→        _: any AudioCaptureSession,
   375→        didUpdateLevels levels: AudioLevels
   376→    ) {
   377→        onLevelsUpdate?(levels)
   378→    }
   379→
   380→    func captureSession(
   381→        _: any AudioCaptureSession,

   383→    ) {
   384→        onError?(error)
   385→    }
   386→
   387→    func captureSession(
   388→        _: any AudioCaptureSession,
   389→        didFinishCapture _: RecordingResult
   390→    ) {}
   391→}

   393→// MARK: - Sendable Weak Reference
   394→
   395→/// A Sendable wrapper for a weak reference to a MainActor-isolated object.
   396→/// Used to safely pass a reference across concurrency boundaries (e.g. CoreAudio callbacks)
   397→/// without triggering Swift 6 actor isolation traps.
   398→private final class WeakSendableBox<T: AnyObject>: @unchecked Sendable {

No good split here. The structure is:

- **Lines 1–356**: `RecordingService` — one coherent class, everything inside it is tightly coupled state
- **Lines 358–391**: `CaptureDelegateAdapter` — private, exists only to serve `RecordingService`, moving it to a separate file would obscure that relationship
- **Lines 393–403**: `WeakSendableBox` — generic utility, but it's 11 lines

The only *honest* candidate would be `WeakSendableBox` since it's generic and could theoretically be reused. But for 11 lines it's not worth a separate file, and right now nothing else uses it.

`checkBluetoothRoutingConflict` + `defaultOutputDeviceUID` (lines 304–355) are self-contained enough conceptually, but they mutate `RecordingService` state (`onBluetoothConflict`) so they can't leave without taking the callback with them — not a real separation.

**Verdict:** bump the warning threshold to 450, leave the file intact. The 3 extra lines are from our legitimate feature work, not bloat.

Want me to make that `.swiftlint.yml` change + gate `swift-test` on `swift-lint` passing, all in this PR?

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' }}

jobs:
  changes:
    name: Detect changes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: read
    outputs:
      mac: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.mac }}
      core: ${{ github.event_name == 'push' || steps.filter.outputs.core }}
      has-cargo: ${{ steps.cargo-check.outputs.exists }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@de90cc6fb38fc0963ad72b210f1f284cd68cea36 # v3.0.2
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            mac:
              - 'mac/**'
            core:
              - 'core/**'
      - name: Check for core/Cargo.toml
        id: cargo-check
        run: |
          if [ -f core/Cargo.toml ]; then
            echo "exists=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "exists=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

  swift-lint:
    name: Swift Lint
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.mac == 'true'
    runs-on: macos-26
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - name: Install SwiftLint
        run: |
          SWIFTLINT_VERSION=0.63.2
          curl -sL "https://github.com/realm/SwiftLint/releases/download/${SWIFTLINT_VERSION}/portable_swiftlint.zip" -o swiftlint.zip
          unzip -q swiftlint.zip -d swiftlint
          echo "$PWD/swiftlint" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Install SwiftFormat
        run: |
          SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION=0.59.1
          curl -sL "https://github.com/nicklockwood/SwiftFormat/releases/download/${SWIFTFORMAT_VERSION}/swiftformat.zip" -o swiftformat.zip
          unzip -q swiftformat.zip
          chmod +x swiftformat
          echo "$PWD" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: SwiftLint
        run: swiftlint lint --strict --config mac/.swiftlint.yml mac/PabloCompanion/

      - name: SwiftFormat
        run: swiftformat --lint --config mac/.swiftformat mac/PabloCompanion/

  swift-test:
    name: Swift Build & Test
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.mac == 'true' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
    runs-on: macos-26
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - name: Cache SPM dependencies
        id: spm-cache
        uses: actions/cache@cdf6c1fa76f9f475f3d7449005a359c84ca0f306 # v5.0.3
        with:
          path: mac/SourcePackages
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-spm-${{ hashFiles('mac/PabloCompanion.xcodeproj/project.xcworkspace/xcshareddata/swiftpm/Package.resolved') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-spm-

      - name: Build & Test
        run: |
          DISABLE_PKGS=${{ steps.spm-cache.outputs.cache-hit == 'true' && '-disableAutomaticPackageResolution' || '' }}
          xcodebuild -project mac/PabloCompanion.xcodeproj \
            -scheme Pablo \
            -destination 'platform=macOS' \
            -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath mac/SourcePackages \
            $DISABLE_PKGS \
            CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" \
            CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO \
            CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO \
            test | xcpretty

  rust-lint:
    name: Rust Lint
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.core == 'true' && needs.changes.outputs.has-cargo == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
        with:
          components: clippy, rustfmt

      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@779680da715d629ac1d338a641029a2f4372abb5 # v2.8.2

      - name: Check formatting
        working-directory: core
        run: cargo fmt --all --check

      - name: Clippy
        working-directory: core
        run: cargo clippy -- -D warnings -W clippy::undocumented_unsafe_blocks

  rust-test:
    name: Rust Test
    needs: changes
    if: needs.changes.outputs.core == 'true' && needs.changes.outputs.has-cargo == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@779680da715d629ac1d338a641029a2f4372abb5 # v2.8.2

      - name: Test
        working-directory: core
        run: cargo test

  dependency-review:
    name: Dependency Review
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
      - uses: actions/dependency-review-action@05fe4576374b728f0c523d6a13d64c25081e0803 # v4.8.3
        with:
          fail-on-severity: high
          deny-licenses: GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0, AGPL-3.0
